questions.question: In Vue 3.4+, what is the first argument passed to a computed getter function?
Why must you compute the new value BEFORE comparing it with the old one inside a computed getter?
options: Vue requires strict execution order for performance
An early return skips reactive dependency tracking, breaking the computed
The comparison function only works on fully computed values
Vue optimizes the order automatically anyway

questions.question: Vue 3.4+ automatically avoids re-running effects when a computed value hasn't changed. What is the limitation?
options: This only works for computed properties, not watchers
This only works for primitive return values, not objects
This only works in production builds
This requires the deep: true option on computed
